problem.title: Form the Largest Number
problem.description: Given a list of non-negative integers, arrange them such that they form the largest number.
assumptions: Output is a string (may have leading zeros if all are 0).
Custom comparator: a+b vs b+a as strings.

example.explanation: Sort by comparing concatenation: 9>5>34>3>30 when determining which comes first.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Custom Sorting
solutions.description: Convert each integer to a string and sort using a custom comparator that compares two concatenation orders (b+a vs a+b) to determine which number should come first. This works because the relative ordering of any two numbers that maximises the concatenated result is transitive, making the custom sort globally correct. The edge case of all zeros is handled by checking if the first element after sorting is '0'.
